dataset: Deterministic synthetic generation (seed=28) based on real pET-28a(+) MCS enzyme order and human EPO CDS properties

pi_items.source: Parsed LOCUS line from generated pET28a_vector.gb inside live VM: 'LOCUS pET28a_vector 5369 bp DNA circular SYN'. Extracted and counted sequence from ORIGIN section: 5369 bases. Cross-referenced with real pET-28a(+) vector size from Addgene/Novagen documentation (5369 bp). All match.

Parsed LOCUS line from generated epo_insert.gb inside live VM: 'LOCUS epo_insert 582 bp DNA linear PRI'. Extracted and counted sequence from ORIGIN section: 582 bases. Cross-referenced with NCBI NM_000799.4 human EPO CDS length (582 bp encoding 193 amino acids + stop codon). All match.

Verified in the generated GenBank file: misc_feature 158..203 /label='MCS'. Extracted sequence at positions 158-203 (1-based): GGATCCGAATTCGAGCTCGTCGACAAGCTTGCGGCCGCACTCGAGT (46 bp). Confirmed all 7 restriction sites are present within this region. Cross-referenced enzyme order (BamHI-EcoRI-SacI-SalI-HindIII-NotI-XhoI) with real pET-28a(+) MCS from Novagen/EMD Millipore documentation — enzyme order matches.

Verified the results directory exists at /home/ga/UGENE_Data/cloning_design/results/ with correct ownership (ga:ga) and is writable. The export_result.sh script checks for these exact paths. Verified ~ expands to /home/ga for user ga.

Cloning design (selecting restriction enzymes, analyzing vectors, designing insert strategies) is a core molecular biology workflow. Web search confirms pET-28a(+) is the most widely used E. coli expression vector for recombinant protein production.

Recombinant EPO (erythropoietin) is one of the most commercially important biopharmaceuticals, produced by recombinant expression. This task directly models the first step in developing a recombinant protein production system.

pi_items.metadata_value: Deterministic generation based on real pET-28a(+) MCS and human EPO CDS properties
pi_items.verified_value: Deterministic generation based on real pET-28a(+) MCS and human EPO CDS properties
pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh: uses random.seed(28) for deterministic generation. The MCS enzyme order (BamHI-EcoRI-SacI-SalI-HindIII-NotI-XhoI) matches the real pET-28a(+) MCS from Novagen documentation. EPO CDS length (582 bp) matches NCBI NM_000799.4. Backbone sequences are randomly generated, not from NCBI. Data is synthetic but biologically realistic.

privileged_info_summary: The pET-28a(+) vector is 5369 bp circular with 7 unique restriction sites in the MCS (GenBank positions 158..203). The MCS contains, in order: BamHI at position 158 (GGATCC), EcoRI at 164 (GAATTC), SacI at 170 (GAGCTC), SalI at 176 (GTCGAC), HindIII at 182 (AAGCTT), NotI at 188 (GCGGCCGC), and XhoI at 197 (CTCGAG). Each enzyme cuts exactly once in the entire vector. The vector has annotated features including T7_promoter (100..120), lac_operator (121..140), His_tag_N (141..157), MCS (158..203), His_tag_C (204..225), T7_terminator (260..310), f1_ori (450..900), KanR (complement 950..1760), pBR322_ori (2900..3500), and lacI (complement 3600..4680). The EPO insert is 582 bp linear with ATG start codon and TAA stop codon (GC content 48.1%). EPO contains internal HindIII site at position 202 and SacI site at position 403, making these two enzymes INCOMPATIBLE for cloning. EPO does NOT contain BamHI, EcoRI, SalI, NotI, or XhoI sites, making these 5 enzymes COMPATIBLE. Any pair of two different enzymes from {BamHI, EcoRI, SalI, NotI, XhoI} is a valid answer for the directional cloning enzyme pair (10 valid combinations). The approximate recombinant construct size is 5369 + 582 = 5951 bp (exact size depends on the chosen enzyme pair and the small MCS fragment removed during cloning). The agent must use UGENE's 'Find restriction sites' (Actions > Analyze > Find restriction sites) and 'Find ORFs' (Actions > Analyze > Find ORFs) tools, export annotated GenBank files, generate a circular map via the Circular View, export it as SVG, and write a comprehensive cloning report.
